subject: Human Resources
title: Onboarding New Employees
description: A quick win: understand Onboarding New Employees.

hook.text: A new hire's first day: no desk, no laptop, nobody knows they're coming. They quit within a month.

buildup.text: Onboarding is the structured process of integrating new employees into the organization.

discovery.text: Great onboarding covers logistics, culture, role clarity, and relationship building—not just paperwork.

twist.text: Companies with strong onboarding improve new-hire retention by 82%. Most companies still wing it.

climax.text: The first 90 days shape whether someone becomes a long-term contributor or a quick departure.

punchline.text: First impressions last. Onboarding is that impression.

quiz.question: What does effective onboarding include beyond paperwork?
options: Culture, role clarity, and relationship building
